payload.summary: Durable state, queue, artifacts, decisions, and resume hints verified.
Nuo contamination and system-blocker classification contract verified.
Qi AB round passed; next round may be queued.
User-facing mission dashboard can explain status and next step.
Collaboration ticket loop has question, SLA, fallback, response, and resume semantics.
Qi has production-stage capability profiles with evidence, holdout, regression, and rollback notes.
External behavior samples have been distilled into KUN-native evidence and replay profiles.

payload.reason: delivery manifest is ready for acceptance
dogfood acceptance recorded and learning writeback is complete

missions.objective: Make KUN V6 Control Plane run real long tasks with recovery, dashboard, Nuo, Qi, collaboration, and learning.

acceptance_criteria: State can be recovered after process restart.
User can read mission status without terminal logs.
Pollution and environment blockers are invalidated by Nuo before quality scoring.
Frontier50 can run as a Control Plane work item.
Human tickets resume or close work according to SLA/fallback.
Qi capability candidates require replay/holdout/canary/rollback before production.
assumptions: Use local file-backed Control Plane state until database persistence is wired.
High-risk external actions stay behind collaboration tickets.
constraints: Do not optimize OpenClaw, Hermes, or GPT-5.5.
Do not count comparator pollution as KUN capability failure.
Do not promote learned capabilities on speed or cost if result quality fails.
known_facts: Round-02 Frontier50 passed after KUN-only fixes.
AB remains a regression gate while dogfood becomes the main productization path.
OpenClaw/Hermes are behavior samples, not code to copy.
merge_plan: All subsystem outputs merge through ArtifactManifest and GateEvaluation.
User-facing report is generated from progress summary, not raw logs.
task_plans.objective: Make KUN V6 Control Plane run real long tasks with recovery, dashboard, Nuo, Qi, collaboration, and learning.
rollback_plan: Rollback capability candidates by reverting CapabilityProfile promotion.
Rollback runtime changes by restoring previous Control Plane state snapshot.
test_plan: Run AB round regression when capability behavior changes.
Run dogfood recovery by rehydrating FileControlPlaneStore.
worker_plan: control-plane owns state, queue, dashboard, API, and tickets.
nuo owns contamination and environment health.
qi owns AB runner, behavior distillation, and capability evolution.
kun runtime consumes only verified capabilities.

work_items.expected_output: Persist mission state, queue, checkpoints, artifacts, decisions, and resume hints.
Render non-technical mission status, risk, next step, and human-needed state.
Classify stub, fallback, misroute, timeout, EOF, missing report, and missing reviews.
Run Frontier50 as a Control Plane work item with answers, reviews, report, health, and rerun gates.
Close the human/external ticket loop: who to ask, timeout, fallback, resume, and refusal handling.
Promote learned capability only through replay, holdout, shadow, canary, and rollback gates.
Distill OpenClaw/Hermes behavior into KUN-native contracts and tests without source copying.

acceptance_criteria: State can be recovered after process restart.
User can read mission status without terminal logs.
Pollution and environment blockers are invalidated by Nuo before quality scoring.
Frontier50 can run as a Control Plane work item.
Human tickets resume or close work according to SLA/fallback.
Qi capability candidates require replay/holdout/canary/rollback before production.
constraints: Do not optimize OpenClaw, Hermes, or GPT-5.5.
Do not count comparator pollution as KUN capability failure.
Do not promote learned capabilities on speed or cost if result quality fails.
critical_facts: Round-02 Frontier50 passed after KUN-only fixes.
AB remains a regression gate while dogfood becomes the main productization path.
OpenClaw/Hermes are behavior samples, not code to copy.
working_contexts.summary: Dogfood KUN V6 productization as a real long task.
